features.summary: Walmart delivered strong Q4 2024 results with 4.9% sales growth and 10.9% adjusted operating profit growth in constant currency. The company surpassed $100 billion in global e-commerce sales for the first time and gained market share across segments. Walmart US comp sales grew 4% with strong transaction counts, while international sales increased 13% led by Flipkart, Walmex, and China. E-commerce losses improved by over 40% year-over-year due to lower fulfillment costs and improved last-mile efficiency. The company announced plans for significant store expansion with 150+ new US locations over five years and agreed to acquire Vizio to enhance its advertising platform.
features.management_outlook: Management provided FY2025 guidance of 3-4% constant currency sales growth and 4-6% operating income growth, maintaining their framework of growing operating income faster than sales. They expect continued momentum from higher-margin businesses including advertising (which grew 28% to $3.4 billion), marketplace, and membership services. The company plans 650 US store remodels in FY2025 and expects supply chain automation benefits to accelerate in the second half. Management expressed confidence in their omni-channel strategy and ability to serve customers across all income segments while maintaining pricing flexibility.
features.risks: The company faces potential headwinds from persistent category mix pressure as general merchandise remains weaker than food/consumables, with GM weakness expected to continue into FY2025. SG&A deleverage is anticipated due to business mix changes and higher variable compensation. The Vizio acquisition is expected to be slightly dilutive to EPS in the near term due to transaction costs. Economic uncertainty could impact consumer spending patterns, and the company noted variability in guidance ranges given business scale and market conditions.
features.key_drivers: Key growth drivers include supply chain automation reaching 55% of fulfillment centers by end of FY2026, expansion of higher-margin services (advertising, marketplace, membership), and continued e-commerce profitability improvements through route densification and fulfillment cost reductions. Store remodels and new locations are driving market share gains, particularly among higher-income households. International operations, especially India, Mexico, and China, are expected to contribute significantly to growth with the international segment targeting $200 billion GMV by FY2028.
